titleStmt.title: SII 1.27: original edition by Eugen Julius Theodor Hultzsch
No. 27. ROUND THE OUTSIDE OF THE SHRINE OF MAHENDRAVARMEإڑVARA.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded in TEI/EpiDoc according to the DHARMA Encoding Guide for Documentary Editions, version 2

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
إ›rؤ«bhؤپradvؤپjagotrakل¹£itidharaإ›iravara * * * * * * *
prؤپdurbhإ«to mahendraل¸¥ pratinل¹›patigajatrؤپsivؤ«ryyorjjitؤپdyaل¸¥ .
Etattenopakaل¹‡The vihitamanupamaل¹پ rؤپjasiل¹پheإ›varasya
prؤ«tyؤپ nityammahendreإ›varamadhivasatu sthؤپل¹‡urؤ«إ›assurؤپ[dyaل¸¥ . 1*]
lokؤپdityؤپtprasإ«to raل¹‡arasikacamإ«paل¹…kaإ›oل¹£ipratؤپpؤپ
dyandevo rؤپjasiل¹پhastanayamalabhata إ›rؤ«mahendrؤپbhidhؤپnam .
tena إ›rؤ«rؤپjasiل¹پheإ›varagل¹›havihite إ›rؤ«mahendreإ›varesmi
nnؤپvؤپse kل¹›ttivؤپsؤپل¸¥ saha guhapariل¹£anmaل¹‡ل¸چalaiل¸¥ sannidhattؤپm . 2
yaل¹پ rؤپjؤپ rؤپjasiل¹پhastanayamajanayanmedinؤ«vؤ«rasiل¹پho
vل¹›ttairaل¹پhonivل¹›ttaiل¸¥ kل¹›tayugamaparannirmmimؤپل¹‡o mahendram .
tenedaل¹پ rؤپjasiل¹پheإ›varanikaل¹­asamutthؤپpitaل¹پ saprasؤپdo
nityؤپvؤپsammahendreإ›varagل¹›hamumayؤپ sؤپrddhamؤ«إ›o vidhattؤپm . 3
karotu kؤپlؤپntakaraل¸¥ purؤپntako maheإ›varassarvvasurؤپsurؤپإ›rayaل¸¥ .
padaل¹پ sadؤپ * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * [. 4*]
mahendravarmmeإ›varagل¹›hamThe name of the temple, mahendravarmmeإ›varagل¹›ham, is also engraved on either side of the staircase, which leads to the entrance of the shrine. .

(Verse 1.) May the motionless, the lord, the first of gods for ever joyfully dwell in this matchless (temple of) Mahendreإ›vara, which was constructed near (the temple of) Rؤپjasiل¹پheإ›vara by Mahendra, who sprang â€¢ â€¢ â€¢ (from) the chief of the princes of the holy Bhؤپradvؤپja-gotra, from that إھrjita,This biruda of Rؤپjasiل¹پha occurs also in No. 25, niche 2. whose bravery frightened the elephants of rival kings!

(2.) May the skin-robed together with the troops of his attendants, the Guhas, be present at this dwelling, (called) the holy Mahendreإ›vara, which was constructed (near) the temple of the holy Rؤپjasiل¹پheإ›vara by the illustrious Mahendra, the son of king Rؤپjasiل¹پha, who sprang from that Lokؤپditya (i.e., the sun of the world), whose valour dried up the army of Raل¹‡arasika, just as the heat of the sun does the mud!

(3.) May ؤھإ›a together with Umؤپ graciously take for his permanent dwelling this temple of Mahendreإ›vara, which was erected near Rؤپjasiل¹پheإ›vara by Mahendra, the son of king Rؤپjasiل¹پha, the lion among the heroes of the earth, who produced another Kل¹›ita age by his sinless conduct!
(4.) May Maheإ›vara, the refuge of all gods and demons, who puts an end to time and has made an end of (the demon) Pura, always (take up) his residence â€¢ â€¢ â€¢ â€¢
Digital edition of SII 1.27 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
